avalancheActivity.comment:
Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will continue to be well consolidated and stable. Greatest remaining expanses will be found on North through East to South-East aspects above 800 metres in the higher coires. Other aspects are largely snow free.
The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: It is expected to be a dry day, with some cloud. South Westerly winds, will slowly strengthen throughout the period from Fresh to Very Strong. Freezing level will remain above the summits throughout.
Observed Weather Influences: A stunning dry and clear day. Winds were light and variable on the tops. After a cold night the summit temperatures were just below freezing, but rose through the day to 5 to 6 degrees Celsius.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is gently thawing and well consolidated. Greatest remaining snow amounts can be found above 800 metres, on North through East to South-East aspects, mainly in the higher coires. These snow patches were firm and icy in the morning, before softening slightly during the day. Northern aspects remaining firm. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will remain well bonded and stable throughout. Shaded aspects will be generally firm, whereas slopes exposed to any direct sunlight will develop a softer surface layer. Snow patches and ribbons will remain in depressions and hollows below 950 metres. Remaining cornices could potentially collapse, especially were affected by the sun. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Dry throughout the forecast period with some sunny spells expected during the day.  Fresh Southerly winds will increase to strong during the day and veer to become South-Westerly. The freezing level will remain above the summits.
Observed Weather Influences: A sunny day with light Southerly winds. After cooler temperatures during the night the freezing level rose to above the summits by dawn.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is firm well bonded and stable throughout. Shaded aspects are particularly firm, slopes exposed to direct sunlight have a softer surface layer. Significant glide cracks were noted on a North aspect at 1100 metres. Rockfall was observed on the same aspect.  Snow patches and ribbons of well bonded snow remain in depressions and hollows below 950 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will be stable and well bonded throughout. Shady aspects will remain firm with sun exposed areas developing soft surface layers. Greatest snow amounts will be found on high plateaus and North-West through North to East facing coires. Gradual snow loss at lower elevations. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A dry day with moderate to fresh winds from the South veering South-West in the afternoon. The freezing level will remain above the highest summits.
Observed Weather Influences: A dry, mild and sunny day. Light winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing snowpack continues to consolidate in the mild conditions. Refreezing during the evening and gradually softening during the day with the exception of high shady locations. Snow stability is generally good in all locations. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will continue to slowly consolidate and stability will be good. The surface will become firm overnight, softening during the day. Remaining cornices will be prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: High pressure continues to bring dry and settled conditions. There will be partly clear skies overnight, becoming cloudy during the day with sunny spells. The freezing level will be above the summits. Moderate Southerly winds.
Observed Weather Influences: Dry throughout the forecast period. Clear skies overnight resulted in a surface frost to lower elevations. Sunny during the day with the freezing level above the summits and light variable winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is continuing to consolidate and stability is good. Surface layers refroze overnight and then softened during the day on southerly aspects while remaining mostly firm on Northerly slopes. Any remaining cornices became more unstable as the day progressed. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will remain well bonded and stable. The snow surface will remain very firm in the shade, softening only on sun exposed slopes. Some large cornices will remain, with the possibility of cornice collapse where in direct sun. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: High pressure conditions will bring another mild, dry day. Moderate Southerly winds will increase slightly through the day and veer South-Westerly. The freezing level will be above the summits although some terrain will remain frozen.
Observed Weather Influences: A mild, dry sunny day with light winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is well bonded and stable. The snow surface is very firm in the shade, softening in the sun. Some large cornices are present and are softening where exposed to the sun. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will re-freeze and remain well bonded and stable in all locations and elevations. The greatest continuous snow amounts will exist mainly on North to Easterly aspects above 850 metres patchy cover below this level. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: High pressure continues to bring dry and settled conditions with partly clear skies overnight producing some patchy frost in sheltered glens . Freezing level around 1500 metres at first but rising to over 2000 metres during the day. The light South-Easterly winds will veer through South, strengthen becoming moderate South-Westerly.
Observed Weather Influences: After the cooler overnight temperatures it was a glorious day. Light South-Easterly winds with plus 4 degrees celsius on the higher summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack has consolidated at all levels and is firm and stable with an icy surface. The greatest continuous snow amounts exist mainly on North to Easterly aspects above 850 metres patchy cover below this level. The avalanche hazard is Low.
